title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: St. Patrick's Day Parade in South Boston began at Andrew Square on March 15, 2026, with large crowds.
Participants included Scott Poce of East Bridgewater, who painted his eyebrows green for the event.
Kathy Luginbuel and Teresa Rogers traveled from Florida to join the parade with the Fascinators of Tampa Bay.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Two men, William Sarnie and Andre Silvia, were arrested for cocaine trafficking following a fatal overdose investigation on Cape Cod.
Sarnie faces charges in Barnstable District Court, while Silvia is involved in a separate drug case in Wareham District Court.
Searches related to the investigation involved police departments from multiple towns, the DEA, and Massachusetts State Police.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and was the musical guest on 'SNL's' March 14, 2026, episode, performing songs from his new album.
This marks Styles' second time in a dual role on 'SNL' following his first appearance in 2019 as host and musical guest.
Ryan Gosling made a surprise appearance to introduce Styles' performance, returning the favor from a previous episode cameo by Styles.

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, former Boston teacher, sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation, followed by five years of supervised release.
Gavin used Discord to exploit minors, engaging in sexualized conversations and collecting explicit images from at least 20 underage girls.
Authorities discovered 147 files on Gavin's phone depicting child sexual abuse; victims lived in US, Canada, and the UK.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Patriots have added multiple key players in free agency, including Romeo Doubs and Alijah Vera-Tucker.
Tyreek Hill, despite legal and injury concerns, could be a valuable deep threat addition for the Patriots.
Patriots linebacker depth could benefit from signing Bobby Okereke, known for his strong defensive performance.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta scored a team-high 24 points with 10 rebounds, leading the Celtics to a 111-100 win over the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum scored 20 points with 14 rebounds but struggled from 3-point range, hitting only 2 of 9 attempts.
The Celtics will face five Western Conference teams, starting with the Suns and Warriors at TD Garden next week.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off their St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert at MGM Music Hall on March 13.
The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown opened for Dropkick Murphys at the Boston-area show.
Highlights included performances by Ken Casey, Kevin Rheault, Jeff DaRosa, and Tim Brennan of Dropkick Murphys.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC's NWSL debut saw over 30,000 fans at Gillette Stadium in a match against Gotham FC.
Gotham FC defeated Legacy FC 1-0, with Esther González scoring the winning goal; a record-tying eight yellow cards were issued.
Legacy FC's aggressive play led to a red card for Bianca St-Georges, highlighting the intensity of the Boston-New York rivalry.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall, celebrating 30 years and kicking off St. Patrick’s Day events in Boston.
The band addressed political issues during their set, notably criticizing ICE and supporting immigrants.
They debuted new material including "Only the Strong" and closed with crowd favorites like "I'm Shipping Up to Boston."

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett is running the Boston Marathon to support the Joslin Diabetes Center and people with Type 1 diabetes.
Diagnosed at age 11, Bennett credits Joslin Diabetes Center for providing him with tools and confidence despite his diagnosis.
Bennett is part of Team Beth Israel Lahey Health, running to advocate for a cure and support fellow Type 1 diabetics.
